title: oxytropis fetida subsp. viscosa
intro: Oxytropis fetida subsp. viscosa, also known as sticky globeflower, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, characterized by its sticky hairs and globular flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Oxytropis fetida subsp. viscosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or infused oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
